topic: A student discovers their teacher follows them on social media
raw_output:
[story 1]

The notification flashed across my screen, a jarring splash of color in the otherwise dull landscape of my evening. It was Professor Albright. A picture of him, smiling faintly, posted on his private account. My jaw tightened. I scrolled through the feed, a slow, deliberate movement that felt heavy with simmering resentment. He’d been monitoring my progress, wasn't he? Every assignment, every slightly off answer. I felt a hot flush rise up my neck, a tight knot forming beneath my collar. I minimized the app abruptly, slamming the laptop shut with unnecessary force. I wanted to find something, anything, to make him look foolish, to disrupt the carefully curated image he projected. The air in my room felt thick, charged with a low, unhappy hum.

[story 2]

I stared at the profile picture, the familiar, benign expression of Mr. Henderson mocking me from the glowing rectangle. A sharp, unpleasant sensation coiled in my stomach. It wasn't anger, exactly; it was a cold, hard resolve. I leaned back in my chair, the plastic groaning under my weight, and began typing a reply to a comment he’d left on a post about a recent school event. My fingers moved with a calculated precision, crafting a response that was meticulously witty, dripping with subtle, pointed observations. When I hit send, a small, almost imperceptible smirk touched my lips. He thought he was in control of the narrative, but I was already rewriting the script. The quiet satisfaction of undermining him was a strange, potent feeling.

[story 3]

The realization hit me like a physical blow. That meticulously organized academic, the one who graded papers with unnerving accuracy, was lurking in my digital space. My hands clenched into fists on the desk, the knuckles turning white. A slow, simmering heat began to spread through my chest, making my breath catch. I didn't want to confront him; confrontation was messy. Instead, I began a silent inventory of his recent posts, cataloging every public gesture, every shared thought. It was a quiet, internal siege. I felt a distinct urge to dismantle his carefully constructed facade, brick by digital brick. A deep, unwavering determination settled over me, a silent promise to make him feel the weight of his own visibility.
